title: Other Computer Related Services

narrative.executive_view: This residual code is heterogeneous: MSP-adjacent installation/support and resale-blended shops appear dominant, with disaster recovery and specialist deployment smaller. Automation can lower ticketing and configuration cost, but platform vendors and zero-touch deployment threaten the project revenue base.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI and automation affect triage, documentation, configuration scripts, monitoring and routine support. Field deployment, hardware handling, recovery orchestration, security judgment and client escalation remain human work, so the input is task compression rather than full technician replacement.
narrative.value_capture: A recurring managed-service or DR contract can retain some efficiency, whereas project, incident and resale-plus-services books face competitive pass-through. Vendor platforms are packaging automation upstream; a small provider must own the account and a contracted outcome to keep savings.
narrative.adoption_timing: MSP benchmark evidence shows AI is helping with repetitive tasks, but it does not establish that most residual-code firms have effective delivery-wide adoption. The selected rate remains below 50%, so timing is positive rather than mechanically zero.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The 705 estimated targets include businesses that many MSP buyers would exclude for low MRR or high resale/project mix. M&A benchmarks reward retention and recurring revenue, so selected multiples are below a high-quality MSP case and assume only limited quality improvement.
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is PRESSURED. Regulated recovery, security and accountable operational continuity can persist, but zero-touch distribution reduces installation work and vendor-native backup/DR platforms can absorb the service layer. This is not a durable claim for the residual population.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: MSP evidence is a proxy: most MSPs classify in sibling NAICS codes, while 541519 is a catch-all. No code-specific owner-age source was found. Revenue mix, MRR, resale pass-through and the actual share of DR versus project work are decisive diligence items.

sources.title: Kaseya 2025 MSP benchmark growth trends
sources.what_it_supports: MSP survey evidence that AI is eliminating repetitive tasks and freeing time for strategy.

sources.title: ConnectWise PSA powered by Asio and Pro launch
sources.what_it_supports: Vendor packaging of AI automation across MSP PSA, RMM, security and data-protection operations.

sources.title: Mayfaire Row B2B professional services acquisition guide
sources.what_it_supports: GF Data MSP multiple ranges and the premium for retention and recurring service revenue.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541519 ($19,543,540,823) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541519 ($42,029,844,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $279,051 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541519); class <5: 8,483 firms, avg revenue/firm $429,327 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $67,190 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 841 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,781,555 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $278,813 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 505 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,678,558 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $575,694 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 526 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,815,287 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,692,592 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 179 firms, avg revenue/firm $40,627,151 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,358,149 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 116 firms, avg revenue/firm $190,265,517 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $29,776,553 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 705
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI-enabled MSP tooling supports automation of repetitive technical and administrative tasks, but physical deployment, recovery accountability and escalation remain.
caveats: MSP evidence is a sibling-code proxy.
Role mix is four-digit 5415, not this residual code.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Scripts and configuration automate; deployment judgment persists.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Mixed technical work includes deployable automation.
Status and scheduling automate; escalation persists.

breakdown_by_role.role: Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Tail mixes physical technicians with administration.

pi_dist.rationale: Only recurring contracted delivery can retain much efficiency; project and resale books pass savings through.

pi_moat.rationale: Integrated operational tooling and account knowledge create modest switching costs, but vendors package the automation upstream.
caveats: Platform evidence is not a small-firm moat measure.

t50_years.rationale: Observed task benefits support progress, but current effective adoption is below 50% for this heterogeneous proxy population.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Kaseya reports meaningful task benefit but not delivery-wide penetration; discounted effective use is below 50%.

historical_analogs.value: RMM, PSA and scripted deployment already automated routine IT operations; AI extends that automation into triage and documentation.
historical_analogs.rationale: ConnectWise describes AI-powered automation embedded in core MSP workflow tooling.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Incremental automation to broader agent-assisted operations

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: No code-specific owner-age source was found.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No documented 541519 succession shortage was found.
caveats: Absence is not proof of no firm-level need.

active_consolidators.rationale: MSP deal evidence supports an active but quality-screened buyer pool.

buy_mult.rationale: Below the 6x-7x well-run-MSP median because residual firms can be project/resale-heavy.
caveats: MSP benchmark is a scope proxy.

exit_mult.rationale: Assumes improved contracted recurring mix, within the cited high-quality MSP range.
caveats: Expansion requires evidence of MRR and retention.

pricing_structure.value: Mixed project, incident, resale-plus-services and recurring managed/DR revenue; recurring contracts retain more automation savings.
pricing_structure.rationale: Benchmark and M&A evidence distinguish recurring MSP quality from project work.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Project/resale dominated to recurring managed-service dominated

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Task exposure and adoption are separate: role fractions measure automatable tasks; the below-50% adoption estimate produces positive t50.

terminal_value.justification: Accountable recovery and security work can survive, but zero-touch deployment and vendor-native platforms pressure the modal installation/support revenue base.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: MSP sources are explicitly treated as sibling-code proxies, not direct 541519 observations.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Project and resale work pass automation savings to clients; this is modeled in pi_dist, not counted again in task exposure.

dataset_mismatch.description: The role mix is four-digit 5415 and receipts predate current AI/DRaaS shifts.
sensitivity: A field-technician-heavy six-digit mix would lower task exposure.
Post-shift revenue mix could change labor share.

market_boundary.selection_basis: Residual NAICS classification and source proxies make this a hypothesis; sibling MSP evidence is discounted.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Timing changes materially if effective adoption crosses 50%.
Capture varies with recurring versus project revenue.
Exit depends on proven MRR improvement.

reviewer_note: Diligence the revenue split first: a recurring, regulated continuity operator differs fundamentally from a project/resale installer despite shared residual classification.

A.arithmetic: Python v3.1.2: A = 10 when t50=0 else min(10,10/t50); t50=2, A=5
